rationale: Exact no-regression guardrail: on questions whose querying key authored no candidates, echo demotion must be a byte-stable no-op (Kendall tau = 1 exactly). Any value below 1 means demotion touched an unaffected query.
Same exact no-op guardrail for the demotion-adjusted-mass arm: with no demoted candidates every cluster's mass equals its raw similarity mass, so ordering must match baseline exactly.

Exact no-op guardrail for the P6 pool arm's FLAT surface: the clustering pool feeds only the clustered summary, so flat order must match baseline exactly on unaffected questions (Layer A proves the same no-leak invariant mechanically). Added with the 2026-07-17 pool variant; measured exactly 1.0.

Retrieval-quality floor on the clean corpus. Calibration run 2026-07-16 (local:bge-small-en-v1.5) measured 0.9237, unchanged by the 2026-07-17 fidelity refactor (flat metrics kept their meaning); 0.05 margin absorbs cross-platform ONNX float drift near ties without letting a real retrieval regression pass.

Polluted-arm baseline canary — documents the pollution gap the harness exists to close (clean 0.9237 vs polluted 0.7883, 2026-07-16, flat-metric meaning unchanged 2026-07-17). A drop below 0.74 means retrieval quality regressed beyond the known pollution cost.

Fixture-integrity tripwire, not a quality goal: with demotion OFF, echoes must still dominate the flat top-3 on affected questions (measured 0.8333 on 2026-07-16, flat-metric meaning unchanged 2026-07-17). If a corpus edit de-pollutes the dataset, the demotion metrics below would pass vacuously — this bound fails the run instead.

Display-stage twin of the fixture tripwire, added in the 2026-07-17 recalibration for the production-shaped display call (perPage 20): baseline's #1 displayed cluster measured 0.7423 echo, versus 0.5429 under the old idealized perPage-100 clustered call — so this bound also catches an accidental reversion of the runner to the idealized call shape.

The demotion mechanism's flat-stage effect: same-key session echoes must clear the top-3 on affected questions. Measured 0.0 on 2026-07-16 (from 0.8333 at baseline), unchanged 2026-07-17; 0.1 headroom tolerates one near-tie inversion across the 4 affected questions.

Demotion must surface genuine cross-agent judgments, not merely bury echoes. Measured 0.6617 with echo-on (0.4867 at baseline) on 2026-07-16, flat-metric meaning unchanged 2026-07-17; floor set one near-tie below the measured value.

Echo demotion must not cost graded relevance overall. Measured 0.8171 on 2026-07-16 (above the 0.7883 baseline — demoting grade-1 echoes lifts grade-3 durables), unchanged 2026-07-17; 0.05 margin below measurement.

Recalibrated 2026-07-17 for the production-shaped display call (perPage 20 — the fidelity refactor that changed this metric's meaning): with demotion on, echoes fall out of the 20-window entirely, so the #1 displayed cluster measured 0.0 echo (baseline 0.7423). The previous idealized-call bound was 0.3 against a measured 0.2019; the production-shaped bound is far tighter because the mechanism is stronger at the real window size. 0.1 headroom for near-tie inversions.

The §3d lever must never leave MORE echo in the #1 displayed cluster than member-count ordering does. Recalibrated 2026-07-17 (production-shaped display call): both orderings measure 0.0 — with demotion clearing the 20-window, mass has nothing to flip on this set; the lever's mechanism is proven by the Layer A waterfall test, and its material effect is measured on the real polluted golden export.

Diversity guardrail paired with the relevance metrics (utility x surprise ruling: never gate on relevance alone). Recalibrated 2026-07-17 for the production-shaped display call: measured 0.6893 (was 0.6393 under the idealized call); floor one aspect-slot below measurement.

Regression ceiling for the P6 pool code path, added 2026-07-17: fixed:100 re-admits demoted echoes into the clustering pool (they cluster together and can win exemplar slots), measuring 0.2019 in the #1 displayed cluster versus 0.0 for page-mode demotion on this set. The lever's diversity-gain prediction is deliberately UNGATED here: this 45-trace fixture is smaller than the 100-pool, so the arm degenerates to a whole-corpus pool (aspectCoverage 0.6393 vs 0.6893 page mode) and cannot test the prediction — that measurement belongs to the real golden export sweep (recipe 4431f077).
